title: Weekly Vessel Valuations Report, November 18 2025
summary:
Main
in Weekly Vessel Valuations Report 19/11/2025
Image reference: 2025-11-19_weekly-vessel-valuations-report-november-18-2025_img1.png
Bulkers: VV Bulker values have mostly remained stable this week, with older tonnageseeing an increase due to sales such Seacon Africa
Capesize (Newcastlemax) Seacon Africa (206,300 DWT, Jun 2006, Imabari) sold to Tianjin Global Offshore Engineering for USD 22.7 mil, VV Value USD 21.67 mil
Kamsarmax BC New Ascent (82,200 DWT, Aug 2012, Tadotsu Tsuneishi) sold to unknown Greek buyers for USD 19 mil, VV Value USD 18.7 mil
Supramax BC Eternity SW (58,500 DWT, Feb 2011, Tsuneishi Cebu) sold SS/DD due to unknown Chinese buyers for USD 15 mil, VV Value USD 15.04 mil
Handy BC Yangtze Flourish (32,500 DWT, May 2012, Jiangmen Nanyang) sold to Unknown Chinese buyers for USD 9.9 mil, VV Value USD 10.96 mil
Tankers: VV Tanker values remain mostly stable this week as the S&P and newbuilding markets remaining active with two MR en bloc deals reported this week
4x MR2 (Chemical/Product) (50,000 DWT, 2014, Hyundai Mipo) sold in an en bloc deal to Torm for USD 128 mil, VV Value USD 125.01 mil
4x MR2 (49,800 DWT, 2026-2027, Jingjiang Nanyang) sold to Scorpio Tankers
